title: Bastion proxy rewrite causes split terminal fragments and missing final sentences on SSE resume
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinLedger) reports intermittent loss of the final sentence for long SSE streams when an upstream bastion performs a short connection reset and header rewrite. The terminal JSON chunk often appears split across the reset boundary and the client receives an incomplete closing fragment (missing...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinLedger) reports intermittent loss of the final sentence for long SSE streams when an upstream bastion performs a short connection reset and header rewrite. The terminal JSON chunk often appears split across the reset boundary and the client receives an incomplete closing fragment (missing end_event / closing bracket).

Impact:
- ~0.6% of long (>20s) streaming responses truncated during high load windows, causing client-side retries and duplicate prefixes.
- Affected flows: interactive chat completions and long-form document generation.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool in Redwood hosted prod (us-east-1). Model pinned: redwood-3b-chat v2026-02-28.
- Customer uses a managed bastion that rewrites headers and proxies HTTP/1.1 <-> HTTP/2. Bastion performs idle probes and occasionally injects a content-length header when replaying buffered data.

Steps attempted to ...

title: Variant redispatch to secondary model during staged canary due to orchestrator snapshot lag
preview: Issue summary: During a staged canary rollout for redwood-llama-7b-v2 in us-east, ~3% of BrightHealth AI traffic was unexpectedly routed to the secondary variant (quantized fallback) despite an explicit tenant model pin. Impact: customer reported mixed responses in production affecting 12 tenant sessions (chat flows...
text:
Issue summary: During a staged canary rollout for redwood-llama-7b-v2 in us-east, ~3% of BrightHealth AI traffic was unexpectedly routed to the secondary variant (quantized fallback) despite an explicit tenant model pin. Impact: customer reported mixed responses in production affecting 12 tenant sessions (chat flows). Symptoms observed at 09:21 UTC: inconsistent model outputs from the same session-id, and increased token latency (p50 -> p95).

High-level hypothesis: orchestrator snapshot application lag caused a partial control-plane commit where edge proxies had stale routing snapshots while the central router had already committed a canary weight update. This led to a redispatch path where requests that should have be pinned were re-evaluated by a reconciliation step and dispatched to the secondary variant.

Requested outcome: restore full pin coherence for affected tenant(s), ident...

title: Private: scheduled-worker service tokens receive 403 after STS realm claim contains slash
preview:
Issue summary:
NovaAnalytics reports that scheduled background workers began failing with 403s when calling Redwood Private admin APIs after their internal STS rotation. Impact: dozens of scheduled jobs failing across environments, customer unable to run nightly ingestion.

text:
Issue summary:
NovaAnalytics reports that scheduled background workers began failing with 403s when calling Redwood Private admin APIs after their internal STS rotation. Impact: dozens of scheduled jobs failing across environments, customer unable to run nightly ingestion.

Impact:
- Customer production scheduled workers returning 403 on admin/model endpoints.
- Jobs retried, causing backlog and higher cost.
- Affects all workers that use service tokens issued by customer's STS.

Environment:
- Redwood Private (on-prem bundle v1.6.2) deployed in NovaAnalytics datacenter (prod-us-east).
- Identity stack: internal STS -> ingress-proxy -> redwood identity-proxy -> private control plane.
- Auth scheme: bearer token with JWT including custom "realm" claim used by customer's RBAC mapping.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Obtain service token from customer's STS for job-runner service account (token ...

title: Console alert duplication and incorrect attribution after hourly retention compaction
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer KappaHealth reported multiple duplicate alert notifications for the same console rule over a four-hour window after our hourly retention compaction job ran. Alerts were emitted with different attribution metadata (org/route/model) and caused duplicated paging to their on-call.

text:
Issue summary:
Customer KappaHealth reported multiple duplicate alert notifications for the same console rule over a four-hour window after our hourly retention compaction job ran. Alerts were emitted with different attribution metadata (org/route/model) and caused duplicated paging to their on-call.

Impact:
- Several duplicate P1 alerts sent to KappaHealth on-call (SMS + PagerDuty) between 02:00–06:00 UTC on 2026-03-10.
- Noise led to escalation and temporary disruption of their incident response.
- Affects alert evaluation and attribution reports in Console for orgs with high-cardinality tags.

Environment:
- Production (us-east-1), console and alerting clusters behind ALB.
- Retention compaction job v2.3.1 (daily/hourly rollups) ran at 02:00 UTC.

Steps to reproduce (internal):
1) On a high-cardinality org with many tag permutations, enable a per-route 5m error-rate alert with 1m ...

title: Cascading API-key handovers causing tenant concurrency exhaustion and intermittent 429 bursts
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a burst of 429 responses across their tenant after an automated API key rotation during a high-throughput streaming upload window. The pattern is reproducible when their client rotates keys and simultaneously spawns short-lived prefetch workers that fan out requests to multiple routes...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a burst of 429 responses across their tenant after an automated API key rotation during a high-throughput streaming upload window. The pattern is reproducible when their client rotates keys and simultaneously spawns short-lived prefetch workers that fan out requests to multiple routes.

Impact:
- LexaHealth observed ~30% request failures (429) for 7–12 minutes after an automated key rotation during a nightly data push.
- Business impact: ingestion pipeline stalled; downstream throughput SLA missed.

Steps to reproduce (provided by customer):
1) Configure two API keys (K-A primary, K-B rotated-in) with equal privileges under same tenant.
2) Client spawns 40 short-lived prefetch workers that request streaming completions and embeddings concurrently (burst over ~10s).
3) Client triggers automated rotation: start sending requests with both K-A and K-B for a...

title: Reserved pool throughput degradation caused by GPU power-capping + fragmented micro-batching
preview: Issue summary: Customer BeaconAI observed sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504 timeouts during peak windows on their dedicated reserved pool. Impact: Customer reports request timeouts and model responses taking 2x-4x expected time; production product is degrading for end users. Timeline: proble...
text: Issue summary: Customer BeaconAI observed sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504 timeouts during peak windows on their dedicated reserved pool. Impact: Customer reports request timeouts and model responses taking 2x-4x expected time; production product is degrading for end users. Timeline: problem first reported 2026-03-10 08:10 UTC, support triaged and opened this ticket. Initial hypothesis included noisy-tenant and autoscaler undershoot. This ticket captures investigation showing a combination of node-level GPU power capping (thermal/clock throttling) combined with workload micro-batching fragmentation and a conservative autoscaler min_replicas setting.

title: Intermittent timeouts and doubled latency for repeated prompts: stale prefix / KV cache misses during streamed retry path
preview: Issue summary: Customer (AtlasWrite, Dedicated) reports intermittent per-request latency spikes (1.8-2.5x baseline) and occasional streaming timeouts when users resubmit identical prompts or when client SDK retries a broken stream. Impact: Several customer-facing flows (autosummarize & chat history replay) experienc...
text: Issue summary: Customer (AtlasWrite, Dedicated) reports intermittent per-request latency spikes (1.8-2.5x baseline) and occasional streaming timeouts when users resubmit identical prompts or when client SDK retries a broken stream. Impact: Several customer-facing flows (autosummarize & chat history replay) experience user-facing slowdowns and ~5-10% request failures during peak traffic windows. Environment: prod/us-east, dedicated pool id d-pool-12. Observed primarily on gptx-13b-quantized and gptx-8b-finetune. Steps to reproduce: 1) Send a multi-turn conversation with a repeated prompt token prefix. 2) Force a transient stream disconnect on the client to trigger retry logic (SDK exponential backoff). 3) Observe server-side logs and latency metrics for repeated prompt ID. Expected behavior: Repeat prompt should hit warmed prefix/KV cache and return near-baseline latency with streaming...

title: Blended invoice missing per-workload rate memo for mixed streaming+batch usage
preview: Issue summary: Customer BrightScale.ai received March invoice with a single blended line item labeled 'multi-workload blended rate' but there is no memo breakdown showing how streaming vs batch embeddings usage were apportioned or which rate tiers applied. Impact: customer cannot reconcile costs against internal cha...
text: Issue summary: Customer BrightScale.ai received March invoice with a single blended line item labeled 'multi-workload blended rate' but there is no memo breakdown showing how streaming vs batch embeddings usage were apportioned or which rate tiers applied. Impact: customer cannot reconcile costs against internal chargebacks and is requesting detailed memo lines or provisional credit. Environment: production billing run for billing period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28 covering hosted and dedicated fallbacks across us-east and eu-west. Customer says invoice total matches expectation but line-item explanation is insufficient for their finance team.

title: Mid-cycle per-model rounding caused multi-line invoice delta and provisional credit hold
preview: Issue summary: Customer reporting a $4,312.57 discrepancy across three invoice line items after they changed reserved capacity and model variant pins mid-billing-window. Impact: Customer billing shows duplicate-looking microbatch line items with fractional token rounding that sum to an unexpected delta. Provisional ...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reporting a $4,312.57 discrepancy across three invoice line items after they changed reserved capacity and model variant pins mid-billing-window. Impact: Customer billing shows duplicate-looking microbatch line items with fractional token rounding that sum to an unexpected delta. Provisional credit that normally applies during postback reconciliation was withheld pending investigation.

Context: Novus Retail migrated a portion of traffic from mixlm-13b-v1 to redwood/quantized-medium-7b on 2026-03-08 at ~18:40 UTC to reduce cost. They are on a Dedicated contract with hourly reserved capacity. The pricing tier change happened roughly in the middle of the hourly accumulation window which led to blended per-token rates for the same API key. The customer expects the platform to attribute usage to a single effective tier and apply smoothing/rounding consistently; ins...

title: Edge failover + auth rotation causes missing metadata envelope and trailing paragraph silence on streaming resume
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent incomplete final metadata and a silent trailing paragraph when their websocket or SSE stream resumes after an edge failover/auth cookie rotation. The response body contains the content tokens but the final metadata envelope (terminal JSON object containing stream-level fi...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent incomplete final metadata and a silent trailing paragraph when their websocket or SSE stream resumes after an edge failover/auth cookie rotation. The response body contains the content tokens but the final metadata envelope (terminal JSON object containing stream-level fields like 'id'/'usage'/'choices' metadata) is sometimes missing or cut and the last paragraph is not emitted, causing the client SDK to throw a parse error or appear to hang.

Impact:
- Several Inkwell Media users see a parse error or a UI spinner after a reconnect; last paragraph (summary) often never appears.
- Occurs ~0.5-1% of long-running streaming sessions for this customer during peak traffic.
- Affects critical editor flows where the final paragraph contains a license blurb.

Environment:
- Customer: Inkwell Media (dedicated account)
- Region: us-east
- Model: redwo...

- Customer-facing latency p99 increased from ~220ms to >2...
text:
Issue summary:
DataScribe reported a sudden drop in dedicated pool throughput in us-west-2 starting 2026-03-10 14:10 UTC. Customer traffic experienced elevated p50/p95/p99 for batched routes; many short requests were queued and not scheduled to GPUs.

Impact:

- Throughput dropped ~40% for the reserved pool attached to DataScribe's dedicated capacity.
- Automatic fallback to lower-tier variants triggered repeatedly, causing billing and quality regressions.

- Dedicated pool: ds-prod-reserved-17 (us-west-2)

- Autoscaler: adaptive-v3

Steps to reproduce (from customer):

text:
Issue summary:
Customer (CloudNote Labs, dedicated) started seeing a sudden increase in 5xx responses for text-generation API calls beginning ~2026-03-10 01:18 UTC. Errors are intermittent but sustained in bursts over 10-30 minute windows.

Steps to reproduce (observed):

title: Streaming tool/function output tokens billed on final invoice despite console estimate showing zero
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that streaming requests which include function/tool calls produced console estimates showing 0 tool-output tokens (console estimate column), but the finalized invoice contains token charges attributed to the tool outputs. Impact: LexiHealth reports a $9,200 unexpected charge on their ...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that streaming requests which include function/tool calls produced console estimates showing 0 tool-output tokens (console estimate column), but the finalized invoice contains token charges attributed to the tool outputs. Impact: LexiHealth reports a $9,200 unexpected charge on their monthly invoice tied to streamed sessions that used function calls for structured data retrieval. Environment: Production, us-east-1, enterprise account with reserved capacity and hosted fallback enabled. This affects streaming chat endpoints where the model returns tool invocations (function calls) and the tool outputs are streamed back as separate chunks.

Expected behavior: Console and estimate API should include a consistent token estimate for tool outputs (either zero if non-billable or the same token estimate used for invoice attribution). Final invoice should not int...

title: Halogen Energy — fanout stall causing streaming silence, duplicate responses, and invoice exposure (post-incident follow-up)
preview: Issue summary: During a high-concurrency scheduled batch for Halogen Energy (enterprise, prod us-east), the request fanout path experienced a sustained stall resulting in partial streaming responses followed by duplicate completions. Impact: 11% of in-flight user sessions experienced either (a) a streaming silence l...
text: Issue summary: During a high-concurrency scheduled batch for Halogen Energy (enterprise, prod us-east), the request fanout path experienced a sustained stall resulting in partial streaming responses followed by duplicate completions. Impact: 11% of in-flight user sessions experienced either (a) a streaming silence lasting 6–18s before a replayed completion, or (b) duplicate completions delivered after client-side retry. Billing exposure: duplicate tokens were recorded for 0.8% of requests between 2026-03-10T09:42Z and 2026-03-10T10:26Z. User-visible effect: client-facing SDKs reported truncated transcripts and duplicated final messages; several Halogen automated workflows misinterpreted duplicates and triggered payment processing retrials. Environment: production, us-east, Dedicated pool: ded-pool-us-east-3, routing policy: capacity-first with multipath fanout to 3 backends. Time wind...

title: session-kv-lease-sticky-causing-resumed-conversation-p99-latency-spike
preview: Issue summary: ClearHealth reported a series of resumed conversational requests (user resumes session after short disconnect) that observe elevated tail latency (p99 ~4.2s) and occasional timeouts, compared to normal p99 ~250ms. This occurs after sessions have been inactive for ~30-90s. Impact: user-facing latency s...
text:
Issue summary: ClearHealth reported a series of resumed conversational requests (user resumes session after short disconnect) that observe elevated tail latency (p99 ~4.2s) and occasional timeouts, compared to normal p99 ~250ms. This occurs after sessions have been inactive for ~30-90s. Impact: user-facing latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts causing degraded UX in production. Behavior is reproducible for certain session IDs and high-frequency resume patterns.

Context: This ticket was opened by Support after customer escalation. Initial triage indicates the serving runtime enters a blocking cold-path when a session key is considered "leased" by another backend shard due to a lease state that does not expire promptly. The behavior started after the kvcache lease change in release v2.18.

title: On-demand delta CSV export with UTC normalization, rotating MD5 manifest, and proof-of-exclusion for deleted records
preview: Issue summary: Customer requests a one-off delta export of their tenant activity as a CSV with strict UTC timestamp normalization, a rotating MD5 manifest for each batch file, and a machine-verifiable 'proof-of-exclusion' artifact listing records deleted since the last snapshot. They need the package for a regulator...
text:
Issue summary: Customer requests a one-off delta export of their tenant activity as a CSV with strict UTC timestamp normalization, a rotating MD5 manifest for each batch file, and a machine-verifiable 'proof-of-exclusion' artifact listing records deleted since the last snapshot. They need the package for a regulatory evidence submission and require deterministic ordering and replayability.

Requirements: (1) CSV delta with canonical UTC timestamps and ISO8601 formatting; (2) each batch file accompanied by an MD5 manifest entry and a signed manifest file (HMAC-SHA256 with their provided per-tenant key); (3) a separate "proof-of-exclusion" file: a compact list of deleted record IDs with per-ID deletion-tombstone hashes and a top-level Merkle-root signed by Redwood to prove non-inclusion in prior snapshots; (4) resume tokens and offsets for replay; (5) timezone normalization mapping note...

title: Tool acknowledgement lost after client-side backpressure causing ambiguous replay and side-effect uncertainty
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that when their client applies aggressive client-side backpressure (pause/resume on the streaming socket) while the SDK/edge is in the middle of a function/tool call, the tool acknowledgement gets lost. Impact: downstream systems observe either duplicate side-effects after replay or n...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that when their client applies aggressive client-side backpressure (pause/resume on the streaming socket) while the SDK/edge is in the middle of a function/tool call, the tool acknowledgement gets lost. Impact: downstream systems observe either duplicate side-effects after replay or never-observed confirmations, leading to billing ambiguity and customer data inconsistencies.

Environment: Prod, dedicated deployment for Nimbus Health in us-east. Python SDK 1.8.2 used from a serverless worker behind an in-house proxy.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Start long-lived streaming session via Python SDK to call external tool via function-calling feature.
2) Trigger a tool execution that emits a confirmation webhook synchronously from the tool handler.
3) Simulate client backpressure by pausing the TCP read loop for ~500-1500ms intermittently (server in CPU-constrained...

title: Reserved pool throughput drop from rapid tenant scatter causing slot thrash and GPU allocation shortfalls
preview: Issue summary: During the customer's recurring midday window we observed a sustained p95/p99 throughput degradation for NovaDocs' reserved pool that coincided with a large number of short-lived tenant connections spreading across many slots (tenant scatter). Impact: customer requests experience 3x p99 increase and b...
text: Issue summary: During the customer's recurring midday window we observed a sustained p95/p99 throughput degradation for NovaDocs' reserved pool that coincided with a large number of short-lived tenant connections spreading across many slots (tenant scatter). Impact: customer requests experience 3x p99 increase and burst 429/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED for batched routes, causing downstream timeouts for user-facing documents ingestion pipelines. Environment: production reserved pool (dedicated) in us-east. Observed starting 2026-03-10 11:40 UTC. Steps to reproduce: 1) Replay customer traffic (small, high-concurrency short-streams) into reserved pool using synthetic tenant generator 2) Observe slot churn and repeated small allocations 3) Watch autoscaler not react quickly due to many ephemeral allocations and scheduler fragmentation Observed behavior: rapid slot attach/detach, high scheduler RPC...

preview:
Issue summary:
Customers see a truncated final output and missing attachment URL when the client performs an SSE stream that upgrades to WebSocket mid-response. The last few tokens (often the attachment link or final punctuation) are dropped and the stream terminates without the terminal metadata envelope.

Impact:

text:
Issue summary:
Customers see a truncated final output and missing attachment URL when the client performs an SSE stream that upgrades to WebSocket mid-response. The last few tokens (often the attachment link or final punctuation) are dropped and the stream terminates without the terminal metadata envelope.

Impact:
- KiteNote (enterprise) reports 12% of long-running generation requests lost last-line attachments during heavy traffic windows.
- Affects production traffic in us-west; some artifacts seen in us-east during cross-region failover.

Environment:
prod - us-west, api gateway behind ALB -> edge proxy cluster -> serving-runtime pool. Customer using SDK-js v1.2.4 with automatic SSE-to-WS upgrade enabled.

Expected behavior:
When a client upgrades an open SSE stream to a WebSocket (preferred for bi-directional control), the server should deliver remaining buffered tokens and the f...

title: Progress-callbacks + client-side retry causing unacknowledged tool executions and billing confusion
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer uses the TypeScript SDK in a serverless worker to issue long-running orchestrations that include multiple tool/function calls per user request. They've enabled progress callbacks (per-action progress events) so their frontend can display live status. Recently they observed duplicate billing f...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer uses the TypeScript SDK in a serverless worker to issue long-running orchestrations that include multiple tool/function calls per user request. They've enabled progress callbacks (per-action progress events) so their frontend can display live status. Recently they observed duplicate billing for tool executions: the backend logs show the tool run completed, but the client timed out waiting for the final progress acknowledgement and retried the whole orchestration, which triggered the tooling to re-execute. The customer is requesting guidance on how to avoid unverified double-executions and how to reconcile charges.

Impact:
- Customer reports 2-8% increase in tooling cost for a specific heavy workflow.
- UI shows transient "stuck" state when progress callbacks are lost; sometimes user retries from browser which results in duplicate downstream effects (e.g., dupl...

title: Differential audit-merge manifest for PSP reconciliation and compliance evidence

Customer (Orchid Bank, enterprise) requires a differential (delta) audit-merge manifest that maps Redwood activity records to their internal PSP transaction IDs for reconciliation and regulator evidence. They want cryptographically verifiable manifests (per-batch HMAC) plus a small sample payload demo...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer (Orchid Bank, enterprise) requires a differential (delta) audit-merge manifest that maps Redwood activity records to their internal PSP transaction IDs for reconciliation and regulator evidence. They want cryptographically verifiable manifests (per-batch HMAC) plus a small sample payload demonstrating mapping logic for two days of high-volume traffic.

Impact:

- High priority due to accounting/legal timelines for transaction dispute windows.

Environment:
- Production (us-east primary, eu-west failover).
- Customer uses Redwood hosted API and writes audit exports to their S3 bucket (s3://orchid-bank-redwood/exports/).

Requested deliverables from Redwood Support/Eng: 
1) A differential audit-merge manifest format...

title: IdP-initiated SLO probe truncates session cookie domain causing re-auth loops and 401s in private VPC
preview: Issue summary: After configuring IdP-initiated SSO for Nimbus Health's Private deployment, periodic IdP single-logout (SLO) probes from their vendor (AuthWave) are observed to cause the Redwood ingress to rewrite the session cookie domain by truncating tenant namespace suffixes. Impact: End-users and service account...
text: Issue summary: After configuring IdP-initiated SSO for Nimbus Health's Private deployment, periodic IdP single-logout (SLO) probes from their vendor (AuthWave) are observed to cause the Redwood ingress to rewrite the session cookie domain by truncating tenant namespace suffixes. Impact: End-users and service accounts intermittently hit 401/reauth loops across the Redwood admin and ingest APIs. Expected: SLO probes should not alter or truncate session cookie domain/path; logout events should be processed without breaking other sessions. Environment details: Private control plane deployed into customer's VPC using edge-proxy v2.1.7, ingress-proxy v3.4.0, auth-bridge v1.9. Deployed certs: internal mTLS + customer-managed IdP metadata via SAML. Observed window: recurring since 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC, roughly every 6–8 hours when IdP health probes run.

title: Preflight safety check requests are being billed against tenant quota and causing 429s for interactive traffic
preview: Issue summary: Customer runs a mixed workload where each interactive chat request is preceded by a lightweight 'preflight' safety/scoring check. After enabling a preflight microservice, FinSight started seeing persistent 429 throttles for low-latency interactive chat traffic. Impact: end-users get immediate 429s for...
text:
Issue summary: Customer runs a mixed workload where each interactive chat request is preceded by a lightweight 'preflight' safety/scoring check. After enabling a preflight microservice, FinSight started seeing persistent 429 throttles for low-latency interactive chat traffic. Impact: end-users get immediate 429s for short chat requests despite overall tenant usage being within expected bounds. The customer reports that each chat interaction produces two quota decrements in the console: one for preflight and one for the actual generation.

Expected behavior: preflight checks are lightweight and should either be exempt from tenant token accounting (internal preflight) or billed at a small fixed cost, not double-counting the real generation request. Observed behavior: preflight + generation both decrement tenant token buckets as full requests, leading to tenant quota exhaustion and 429s ...

title: High attach latency and throughput collapse from small-chunk inference stream CQ saturation
preview: Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach ti...
text:
Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach timeouts and increased fallbacks to smaller model variants. Environment: prod us-west dedicated pool, heterogeneous GPU hosts (A100 80GB + A10 mixed), customer traffic characterized by many short inference streams (~12-32 tokens) at high qps.

We observed a pattern inconsistent with normal autoscaler signals: autoscaler did not trigger additional hosts despite attach latency rising. Support triaged as potential noisy-tenant + kernel-level IO backpressure (PCIe completion queue saturation) affecting attach path and the serving scheduler fairness.

Impact details: Customer repo...

title: Intermittent 5xx wave from TLS-resume-ticket exhaustion combined with HTTP/2 negotiation mismatch between API gateway and runtime
preview: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NimbusHealth observed repeated 5xx errors for short periods (~2-8 minutes) across multiple chat and embedding routes. The API gateway returned 502/503 and upstream serving runtimes reported rapid connection resets and ephemeral worker evictions. Impact: customer-facing re...
text: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NimbusHealth observed repeated 5xx errors for short periods (~2-8 minutes) across multiple chat and embedding routes. The API gateway returned 502/503 and upstream serving runtimes reported rapid connection resets and ephemeral worker evictions. Impact: customer-facing requests intermittently failed (up to 18% error rate during the peak window) causing degraded application experience for their enterprise workflows. Environment: production (us-east), mixed-hosted and dedicated tenant; incident coincided with a tenant priority reconfiguration from NimbusHealth account team.

title: Long-tail prompt length burst causes KV-cache fragmentation, runtime OOMs and elevated 5xx (apigw 503)
preview:

Customer CurrentsAI reported a sudden spike of 503/5xx responses starting 2026-03-10 02:10 UTC for a subset of their requests. Error rate peaked ~18% for the tenant for ~45 minutes.

Impact:
- Tenant-facing latency increased and many streaming sessions were terminated with 503.
- Several serving-runti...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer CurrentsAI reported a sudden spike of 503/5xx responses starting 2026-03-10 02:10 UTC for a subset of their requests. Error rate peaked ~18% for the tenant for ~45 minutes.

Impact:
- Tenant-facing latency increased and many streaming sessions were terminated with 503.
- Several serving-runtime pods experienced OOM kills and restarted; autoscaler reacted by adding nodes which increased churn.
- Customer reports multiple failed inference calls and degraded UX for production users.

Timeline (high-level):
- 02:08 UTC: tenant traffic shows a shift: 90th/99th prompt length jumped from ~400 tokens to ~6k tokens (long-tail burst from batch import job).
- 02:10 UTC: API Gateway backlog increases, 503s observed and routed to support alerts.
- 02:15 - 02:40 UTC: multiple runtime pods report GPU memory fragmentation and OOM; KV cache evictions spike.
- 02:45 UTC: mitigat...

title: Sustained short-window 429s after switching to pooled-proxy signed-url strategy during large multipart embedding import
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiData reports steady stream of 429 responses for an otherwise healthy multipart embedding import that started after they switched to a pooled-proxy + short-lived signed URL callback flow. The errors are concentrated during concurrent callback fanout and appear as sustained short-window thr...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiData reports steady stream of 429 responses for an otherwise healthy multipart embedding import that started after they switched to a pooled-proxy + short-lived signed URL callback flow. The errors are concentrated during concurrent callback fanout and appear as sustained short-window throttles (soft 429s) rather than a single burst.

Impact:
- Production embedding backfill stalled for multiple jobs, causing downstream search freshness degradation for LexiData customers.
- Errors began ~2026-03-09 18:10 UTC and persist intermittently across the affected import windows.

User-reported context:
- They moved from direct single-host uploads to a pooled proxy (customer-side) that multiplexes callbacks to our signed-url endpoint.
- Each signed URL rotation results in ~50 short-lived callbacks at once.
- Happens only in us-east region and only for multipart embedd...

title: SSE stream truncates and resumes with interleaved metadata after edge cache chunk insertion
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers observe SSE streams terminating early (HTTP 200 closed) with a partial JSON payload, then receiving a resumed stream that injects an edge-cache metadata chunk in the middle of the response. The resumed payload has metadata frames interleaved with tokens, which client SDKs mis-interpret and a...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers observe SSE streams terminating early (HTTP 200 closed) with a partial JSON payload, then receiving a resumed stream that injects an edge-cache metadata chunk in the middle of the response. The resumed payload has metadata frames interleaved with tokens, which client SDKs mis-interpret and assemble into malformed JSON.

Impact:
- Multiple API consumers at Foodly see truncated recommendations or garbled JSON in ~2-3% of long streaming responses (conversations > 4k tokens).
- Causing client-side JSON.parse failures and repeated retries. Some retries lead to duplicated prefixes.

Environment:
- Dedicated customer tenant on prod-us-east using dedicated routing through our edge CDN (FastEdge).
- Streaming via SSE, client uses our JS SDK v2.3.1 behind a corporate proxy.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Client issues long-stream generation request (max_tokens ~ 409...

title: Go SDK: idempotency key prefixing by orchestrator leads to duplicate function/tool invocations
preview: Customer reports repeated duplicate downstream tool/webhook invocations originating from a long-running, multiplexed orchestration flow that uses per-action idempotency semantics. The Go SDK + their orchestrator appear to be prefixing the idempotency key with a shard/orch token before each sub-request; in some cases...
text: Customer reports repeated duplicate downstream tool/webhook invocations originating from a long-running, multiplexed orchestration flow that uses per-action idempotency semantics. The Go SDK + their orchestrator appear to be prefixing the idempotency key with a shard/orch token before each sub-request; in some cases the prefixed key is truncated by an edge proxy, resulting in collisions and duplicate side effects.

title: Embedding padding/trim mismatch causing rerank order oscillation and autoscaling surge
preview: Issue summary: AtlasDocs reports intermittent rerank quality regressions (top-k order flips) coincident with brief 500/timeout spikes and a burst of autoscaling activity. Impact: customer search relevance fluctuates for ~5-30s windows, end-users see wrong top results and increased latency. Observed primarily on dedi...
text: Issue summary: AtlasDocs reports intermittent rerank quality regressions (top-k order flips) coincident with brief 500/timeout spikes and a burst of autoscaling activity. Impact: customer search relevance fluctuates for ~5-30s windows, end-users see wrong top results and increased latency. Observed primarily on dedicated pool in us-east. Hypothesis: mismatched embedding vector length from ingest vs reranker (padding vs trim) interacts with prefix caching and leads to CPU fallback and reordering.

title: Tenant webhook audit stream: mutual-TLS client cert rotation, backfill requests, and SOC2 evidence bundle

They need (1) a validated backfill of the missing events for...
text:
Issue summary:
GreenSpring Health uses our tenant-scoped webhook endpoint for real-time audit delivery. During a scheduled client certificate rotation on 2026-03-09 they reported a gap: ~18k audit events (approx. 3 hours) did not arrive at their collector. They need (1) a validated backfill of the missing events for tenant T-GREEN-12 with chronological ordering preserved, (2) cryptographic proof of the server-side signing/checkpoint used during the backfill window, and (3) a signed artifact demonstrating the client cert rotation timeline (previous cert thumbprint, rotation timestamp, and validation that no events were re-delivered outside the window).

Impact:
- Missing audit records for regulated ePHI ingestion pipeline (GreenSpring Health, HIPAA scope).
- Customer cannot complete urgent SOC2 evidence packet for their auditor until audit continuity is proven.

Environment:
- Tenant: ...

title: Proxy chunk boundary splits multibyte UTF-8 grapheme, causing truncated final character on SSE resumes
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an SSE connection resume (client reconnect + resume token) the final character of the streamed completion is sometimes replaced with a Unicode replacement character or missing entirely. Impact: end-user documents produced by LuminaDocs have truncated final punctuation or a ...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an SSE connection resume (client reconnect + resume token) the final character of the streamed completion is sometimes replaced with a Unicode replacement character or missing entirely. Impact: end-user documents produced by LuminaDocs have truncated final punctuation or a corrupted emoji at the end of sentences in ~0.4% of resumed streams, causing downstream validation to fail. Environment: production, dedicated cluster in us-east, requests proxied through customer's on-prem nginx reverse proxy before reaching Redwood edge.

title: Unexpected 5xx spike driven by tenant throttle, accept-queue growth and runtime worker flood

The pattern included high API Gateway accept-queue depth, connection keepalive churn, and repeated runtime worker restarts leading to upstream 502/503 errors.

text:
Issue summary:
Customers observed a sharp rise in 5xx responses across multiple endpoints for a subset of tenants in us-east-1 starting ~2026-03-10T20:05Z. The pattern included high API Gateway accept-queue depth, connection keepalive churn, and repeated runtime worker restarts leading to upstream 502/503 errors.

Impact:
- Lumina Analytics (dedicated) reported increased API failures affecting interactive chat and short-streaming sessions (~30% failure rate for a 5-minute window).
- Broader, low-level 5xx floor visible for ~12 tenants sharing the same routing shard.

Environment:
- prod us-east-1
- Affected routing shard: shard-e-12
- Serving runtime version: 2026.02.11-hotfix

Observed timeline (UTC):
- 2026-03-10T20:03: service-level alarms start triggering on apigw accept-queue (>1500).
- 2026-03-10T20:05: Lumina support case created; we see elevated 5xx percentages at api layer.
-...

title: Console widget load latency / 504s when trace-hook request headers exceed 16KB
preview:
Issue summary: Several LuminaHealth users report Dashboard widgets failing to render or timing out (504) intermittently when the underlying traces include very large trace-hook header payloads (base64-encoded span attributes).

Impact: Affects multiple dashboard panels that include trace links. Charts either hang in...
text:
Issue summary: Several LuminaHealth users report Dashboard widgets failing to render or timing out (504) intermittently when the underlying traces include very large trace-hook header payloads (base64-encoded span attributes).

Impact: Affects multiple dashboard panels that include trace links. Charts either hang in 'loading' state for 15-30s then 504, or render partial data with missing trace links. This prevents on-call and SRE from drilling into traces during incidents and has impacted two production incident investigations in the last 48h.

Environment: Production, us-east-1. Issue started after deployment of trace-hook header expansion change (round-robin agent enrichment) rolled on 2026-03-09. Customer on enterprise plan with Route policy pinned to us-east ingestion.

Observed behavior: When trace metadata headers attached to trace-hook callbacks exceed ~16KB, the console widget...

title: On-demand ordered entitlement-change ledger slice with encrypted manifest and HMAC batch proofs for legal review
preview: Issue summary: Finverity Bank (enterprise) requests a forensically-ordered slice of entitlement-change events for a two-week window (2026-02-22 to 2026-03-07) for legal review. They require per-batch HMAC proofs, an encrypted manifest that lists sequence ranges and checksums, and delivery to their SFTP endpoint with...
text:
Issue summary: Finverity Bank (enterprise) requests a forensically-ordered slice of entitlement-change events for a two-week window (2026-02-22 to 2026-03-07) for legal review. They require per-batch HMAC proofs, an encrypted manifest that lists sequence ranges and checksums, and delivery to their SFTP endpoint with PGP encryption.

Impact: Customer legal team cannot complete subpoena review until they receive verifiable, ordered audit artifacts. This is blocking a regulatory deadline.

Request details: Provide a chronological (event-time ordered) ledger export that includes: subject identifier (pseudonymized per prior agreement), change type (grant/revoke/modify), actor principal, timestamp (UTC), request_id/trace_id, and pre-/post- entitlement state where available. Deliverables must include: 1) NDJSON export slices (max 100k events per file), 2) per-file SHA256 checksum, 3) manifes...

title: Edge NAT handshake interruption results in incomplete numbered checklist + missing embedded image on SSE stream

text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that long SSE streaming responses intermittently terminate with the last numbered checklist item missing and a final embedded image URL omitted. This happens after their users experience a network path change (ISP NAT port refresh / IP rebind).

Impact: Several LuminaDocs customers see corrupted docs: a numbered checklist in the final paragraphs is cut at item 3 of 4 and the final image embed (markdown ![diagram](...)) never arrives. Problem first reported 2026-03-10; frequency ~2-3% of long (>= 2k token) streams for their users.

Environment: production (us-east), customer on dedicated plan with VPC egress through their ISP (residential & corporate). Affected model: gpt-open-xt-4b.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Start SSE request that streams a long markdown document containing a 4-item numbered checklist and an inline image URL near the end.
2) Wh...

title: Scheduled dashboard snapshot suppressed when query includes tracehook + aliased route identifiers
preview:

Scheduled snapshot jobs for dashboards that include a query-level tracehook (tracehook param in the query) are being suppressed and not persisted when the query references aliased route identifiers (route aliases used by the customer).

Impact:
- OrbitPay scheduled daily snapshots for 20 dashboards sh...
text:
Issue summary:
Scheduled snapshot jobs for dashboards that include a query-level tracehook (tracehook param in the query) are being suppressed and not persisted when the query references aliased route identifiers (route aliases used by the customer).

Impact:
- OrbitPay scheduled daily snapshots for 20 dashboards show 'Last run: succeeded' but no snapshot file appears in the scheduled_reports listing and email reports are empty for affected dashboards.
- This affects reporting and SLO visibility for two top-level product teams during their monthly review.
- Customer-visible data loss for scheduled exports (not ad-hoc snapshots).

Environment:
- Prod (us-east-1)
- Tenant: orbitpay-prod (enterprise)
- Dashboards affected are high-cardinality, use route aliasing for multi-tenant routes and include tracehook parameters in WHERE clauses.

Steps to reproduce (minimal):
1) Create dashboard w...

title: QUIC path migration during mobile handoff truncates streaming SSE final fragment via CDN
preview: Customer NoteWave Mobile reports intermittent truncated final sentences and missing terminal metadata on long-lived SSE streams when a mobile device transitions between cellular and Wi-Fi. Issue only observed when requests route through our CDN with HTTP/3 enabled. The stream appears to resume but the final 'choice'...
text: Customer NoteWave Mobile reports intermittent truncated final sentences and missing terminal metadata on long-lived SSE streams when a mobile device transitions between cellular and Wi-Fi. Issue only observed when requests route through our CDN with HTTP/3 enabled. The stream appears to resume but the final 'choice' object and terminal metadata are not delivered to the client, leaving responses incomplete.

title: Avro incremental SIEM delta feed with per-batch Ed25519-signed manifest, tenant retention map, and compliance attachments for EU bank
preview: Customer (NordBank ASA) requests a production-ready incremental SIEM export delivered in Avro container format with the following non-standard requirements: per-batch Ed25519 detached signatures, a machine-readable tenant_retention_map describing per-field retention/TTL and legal-hold flags, timezone-normalized delt...
text: Customer (NordBank ASA) requests a production-ready incremental SIEM export delivered in Avro container format with the following non-standard requirements: per-batch Ed25519 detached signatures, a machine-readable tenant_retention_map describing per-field retention/TTL and legal-hold flags, timezone-normalized deltas (UTC canonical plus local-bank timezone mapping for reconciliation), and a compliance evidence package (sample GLUE schema, checksum manifest, and answers to regulator questionnaire). The feed will be consumed by the bank's Splunk/ELK bridge and must be demonstrably replayable and verifiable for audit. Impact: customer cannot onboard until signed-manifest and retention semantics are validated by their security and legal teams. Request is time-sensitive due to upcoming regulator audit window.

title: Sustained mini-burst shadowing causing gradual GPU credit leakage and p99 throughput spikes
preview: Issue summary: Customer LexiDocs on a dedicated pool is observing gradual throughput degradation at p99 over ~6 hour windows coincident with many short-lived inference streams. Impact: intermittent request spikes, elevated p99 latency (3-10x), occasional 429s and autoscaler failing to add capacity fast enough. Envir...
text: Issue summary: Customer LexiDocs on a dedicated pool is observing gradual throughput degradation at p99 over ~6 hour windows coincident with many short-lived inference streams. Impact: intermittent request spikes, elevated p99 latency (3-10x), occasional 429s and autoscaler failing to add capacity fast enough. Environment: production dedicated cluster in us-west-2 serving the redwood/gpt-4o-instruct-2.7b-quant4x variant. Observed since 2026-03-09 23:30 UTC and recurring daily during customer batch windows.

title: Promotional waiver applied to next billing cycle instead of current after dedicated fallback

Instead, the waiver line appeared as a provisional credit scheduled for the next invoi...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightLearn Inc. reports a promotional waiver (30% overage credit, promo code: SPRINGENTERPRISE-30) intended to offset a disputed overage on the invoice for the current billing cycle (INV-2026-03-BLE-7301). Instead, the waiver line appeared as a provisional credit scheduled for the next invoice period, resulting in a short-term overcharge and an autopay failure for the current cycle.

Impact:
- Immediate financial impact: customer account temporarily charged $12,345.67 more than expected; autopay attempted and failed, triggering billing alerts.
- Business impact: customer procurement flagged the charge and paused integrations until billing is corrected.
- Affects BrightLearn production inference workloads in us-east and eu-west.

Environment:
- Tier: enterprise (dedicated GPU pool)
- Regions: us-east primary, eu-west fallback
- Components implicated: billing po...

title: Sawtooth throttling pattern during customer daily batch sweep causing intermittent 429s
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer ScoutMetrics runs a nightly daily-sweep job that uploads ~10k small embedding requests in bursts every 7 minutes between 02:00-04:00 UTC. Over the last three nights they saw a repeating sawtooth pattern of 429 responses — throughput spikes then a periodic waveform of throttles for ~30s, recov...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer ScoutMetrics runs a nightly daily-sweep job that uploads ~10k small embedding requests in bursts every 7 minutes between 02:00-04:00 UTC. Over the last three nights they saw a repeating sawtooth pattern of 429 responses — throughput spikes then a periodic waveform of throttles for ~30s, recovers briefly, then spikes again. This is affecting their pipelines (15-25% of batches failed), causing downstream retries and significant time-to-completion regression.

Impact:
- Approx 15-25% of batch embedding requests during sweep windows return 429s.
- Customer reports downstream job run time increase from ~12m to ~45m.
- Visible errors in their job logs; customer unable to complete daily indexing reliably.

Environment:
- Tenant: ScoutMetrics (dedicated pool, committed capacity).
- Region: prod-us-east (pool id: pool-ussc-07 provided by customer).
- API path: POST /v1/...

title: SSE: lingered TCP socket results in interleaved token fragments and missing final event on client reconnect
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that after a short mobile network switch the SSE stream resumes but the final 'end' event is missing and some token fragments are interleaved/duplicated. The behaviour is repeatable from the customer's environment when they trigger a cellular->wifi handoff. Impact: Produces incomplete...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that after a short mobile network switch the SSE stream resumes but the final 'end' event is missing and some token fragments are interleaved/duplicated. The behaviour is repeatable from the customer's environment when they trigger a cellular->wifi handoff. Impact: Produces incomplete outputs to end users and causes client-side reassembly failures; NoteFlow reported user-visible truncation in 3 of 10 resumed sessions and data loss for the last paragraph in many cases.

Observed behaviour: When client reconnects following a transient socket drop, the resumed stream sometimes contains duplicated prefix tokens, interleaved heartbeat frames, and the terminal 'event: done' or final JSON marker is not received. Client SDK shows partial last-choice and then the connection closes. Server traces show the backend had emitted the final marker.

Expected behaviour:...

title: Conservative opt-tuning safety net: tenant sees intermittent 5xx and elevated tail latency after enabling kv-prefetch + q4 quant profile
preview:
Issue summary: After enabling a tenant-level optimizer profile that enables aggressive KV prefetch (window=32) and a 4-bit quant profile (q4_mixed) the customer reports intermittent 5xx errors and a noticeable P99 tail latency increase for interactive chat routes.

Impact: Intermittent 5xx responses (approx 0.6% of ...
text:
Issue summary: After enabling a tenant-level optimizer profile that enables aggressive KV prefetch (window=32) and a 4-bit quant profile (q4_mixed) the customer reports intermittent 5xx errors and a noticeable P99 tail latency increase for interactive chat routes.

Impact: Intermittent 5xx responses (approx 0.6% of requests during peak) and P95->P99 jump from ~450ms to ~1.2s for the tenant's primary chat route. Customer reports visible user complaints and warned they may revert to a competitor if unresolved.

Environment: Dedicated pool in prod-us-east serving BrightPost Health tenant. Traffic is primarily short-turn chat (avg seq len 80 tokens). The change was applied during a low-traffic maintenance window two days ago and rolled to 100% tenant traffic 90 minutes after initial canary.

Observed behavior: 1) Spikes of 502/503 with upstream serving-runtime logs showing 'KV_CACHE_MISS'...

title: Midflight proxy upgrade causes websocket handoff to drop terminal JSON object and leave truncated choice
preview: Issue summary: During a high-throughput window on 2026-03-10, AtlasApps reported that long-running WebSocket streams occasionally end with a partial JSON 'choice' object and no terminal metadata (end_event). Client logs show the final JSON object missing closing braces and the 'metadata' field absent. Impact: Severa...
text:
Issue summary: During a high-throughput window on 2026-03-10, AtlasApps reported that long-running WebSocket streams occasionally end with a partial JSON 'choice' object and no terminal metadata (end_event). Client logs show the final JSON object missing closing braces and the 'metadata' field absent. Impact: Several production chat flows returned truncated final sentences, sometimes causing downstream parsers to throw and the customer to re-request completions, doubling token usage and affecting SLAs.

Environment: Production (eu-west), requests routed through our edge proxy pool behind a CDN with active health checks and transparent connection rebinds. A small subset of requests go through an older proxy flavor that performs an in-flight socket upgrade to another edge on backend health probe.

Observed behavior: Client receives partial JSON chunk (last text tokens present, no "end" ...

Over the last two days they observe intermittent sessions that end in a half-closed state: the client receives a final 'data: [DONE]' but the JSON choice is truncated or duplicated and subsequent requests from different browser t...
text: Issue summary: Parcelly calls our streaming SSE chat endpoint from a dedicated cluster. Over the last two days they observe intermittent sessions that end in a half-closed state: the client receives a final 'data: [DONE]' but the JSON choice is truncated or duplicated and subsequent requests from different browser tabs sometimes see fragments from the earlier session.

title: Sustained 429s during sliced ingest with priority class mismatch after dedicated pool migration
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports sustained 429 responses across a set of sliced ingest workers after migrating ingestion to a Dedicated pool. The traffic mix is: low-latency chat calls (priority=high) and large multi-part embedding uploads (priority=background). After the migration, background uploads are intermitten...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports sustained 429 responses across a set of sliced ingest workers after migrating ingestion to a Dedicated pool. The traffic mix is: low-latency chat calls (priority=high) and large multi-part embedding uploads (priority=background). After the migration, background uploads are intermittently exhausting burst credits at the per-route layer and causing opaque 429s for high-priority short calls during peak windows.

Impact:
- ~30% of short chat requests returned 429 between 09:00-11:30 PDT on 2026-03-10 for Streamly AI.
- Customer reports user-facing errors and retry amplification in their orchestrator.
- Business impact: partial degradation of their real-time features during peak ingest windows.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-132-usw
- Region: us-west
- Ingest coordinator version: 2026.02.3
- Rate-limiter service version: 1.14.0-canary

Observed behaviour...

title: API throttler feedback loop causing surge in 5xx errors and streaming session drops for PaperTrail Labs
preview: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC we observed a sustained elevation in 5xx responses from the API gateway for traffic belonging to PaperTrail Labs' dedicated tenant. Errors manifested as HTTP 502/503 from the gateway and abrupt streaming session terminations. Impact: Customer-facing failures for PaperTrai...
text: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC we observed a sustained elevation in 5xx responses from the API gateway for traffic belonging to PaperTrail Labs' dedicated tenant. Errors manifested as HTTP 502/503 from the gateway and abrupt streaming session terminations. Impact: Customer-facing failures for PaperTrail's realtime chat product (multiple streams dropped, reconnect storms). Scale: Affected roughly 6% of the tenant's requests during the spike window (~10:30-11:15 PDT), with short bursts peaking at ~18% error rate. Business impact: user-facing errors and automated client retries resulting in support escalations. Timeline: - 2026-03-10 03:37 UTC: Monitoring alert triggers (gateway 5xx rate increased). - 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC: Support opened ticket; customer reports streaming disconnects. - 2026-03-10 04:05 UTC: On-call SRE and ingress engineer pulled into Slack (#support and #...

title: Streaming chat stalls mid-generation when Dedicated GPU worker triggers kernel memory compaction
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightForms reports that SSE-based chat streams stop producing tokens mid-response (~after 200-600 tokens) but the HTTP connection remains open. The client sees no final event and ultimately times out after ~30s of silence. This is observed only on their Dedicated pool in us-west-2 and primar...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightForms reports that SSE-based chat streams stop producing tokens mid-response (~after 200-600 tokens) but the HTTP connection remains open. The client sees no final event and ultimately times out after ~30s of silence. This is observed only on their Dedicated pool in us-west-2 and primarily with redwood-neo-13B-quant; some occurrences with redwood-14B-v1 were reported after model fallback.

Impact:
- Multiple end users see partial/paused responses in production web chat widgets.
- Reported across three customer sessions in the last 24 hours; affects important SLAs for BrightForms (enterprise contract).
- Workaround (client reconnect) recovers response but may produce duplicated content or truncated context.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity (reserved GPU pool) for BrightForms Inc. in us-west-2
- Serving runtime version: v1.18.3
- Autoscaler: v0.9.1 (scale...

title: Edge proxy half-close results in silent stream tail and missing final tokens for SSE sessions
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that many SSE streams from the hosted API appear to terminate 'silently': the client receives the penultimate chunks and then the connection closes or reuses the underlying TCP connection without the expected final 'end' event or final JSON metadata. The visible symptom is truncated f...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that many SSE streams from the hosted API appear to terminate 'silently': the client receives the penultimate chunks and then the connection closes or reuses the underlying TCP connection without the expected final 'end' event or final JSON metadata. The visible symptom is truncated final tokens and missing choice/finish_reason metadata.

Impact:
- Multiple calls from NimbusAI show incomplete responses for medium-length completions (typically 800-2k tokens).
- Affects production traffic routed through our edge proxy in us-west-2.
- Customer experienced degraded UX in customer-facing chat flows (some messages appear cut off).

Environment:
- Redwood hosted API (production) behind edge proxy (Nginx-based layer) -> api-gateway -> serving-runtime (GPUs).
- Customer traffic class: dedicated pool (reserved capacity).
- Observed primarily on HTTP/1.1 keepalive...
